page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores (4 vCPUs) running at 2.5 GHz, built on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.5 GB per core. For acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA Pascal P100 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 472 GB SSD, and the instance has a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between CPU/memory performance and GPU-driven workloads. The host CPU and memory subsystem show weak to poor performance in standard compute benchmarks, with stress-ng single-core scoring 952.78 (bottom 10%) and multi-core scoring 2375.81 (bottom 25%). Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds falling into the bottom 10% tier. However, the integrated NVIDIA P100 GPU delivers strong performance in specialized workloads. In LLM inference tests using a medium 7B model, the instance achieved top-tier results, processing prompts at 316.25 tokens per second and generating text at 42.76 tokens per second.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.gn5-c4g1.xlarge is its reliance on older CPU and memory architectures, which limits its effectiveness for general-purpose compute, database operations, or high-throughput web serving. However, from a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of a dedicated 16 GB GPU and local SSD storage makes it a viable option for legacy GPU-accelerated tasks and small-to-medium model inference without the premium cost of modern-generation accelerators. It is suited for deep learning inference, moderate parallel computing, and GPU-assisted data processing workloads that do not require high-performance host CPU execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 30.0 GB of RAM, and a 472 GB local SSD. It is equipped with a single NVIDIA Pascal P100 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. While standard CPU and memory benchmarks indicate weak performance, with stress-ng and memory bandwidth scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25% tiers, the instance excels in GPU-driven tasks. Specifically, it achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference for medium 7B models, processing prompts at 316.25 tokens per second. This hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff: limited host CPU capability paired with strong legacy GPU acceleration. It is suited for deep learning inference, GPU-assisted data processing, and small-to-medium model execution where host CPU performance is not a primary bottleneck.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 4 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, a 472 GB SSD, and an NVIDIA P100 GPU. It offers strong LLM inference performance despite lower host CPU benchmarks.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 4 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, a 472 GB SSD, and an NVIDIA Pascal P100 GPU.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance combining dedicated Intel Xeon cores and an NVIDIA P100 GPU for efficient small-to-medium model inference.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 30 GB RAM
NVIDIA Pascal P100 GPU with 16 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
472 GB local SSD storage and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier LLM inference performance for medium 7B models
Suited for deep learning inference and GPU-assisted data processing
